Every $1 Oil Rise May Cost Jakarta Up to Rp 4 Trillion, Economist Warns
body: Economists warn each $1 oil hike may add up to Rp 4T in subsidies as Iran-US tensions threaten inflation and rupiah stability.

title: Gold Eases After Rally, Oil Surge Fuels Global Inflation Fears
body: Antam gold falls Rp 13,000 to Rp 3,122,000 as oil surges and Strait of Hormuz tensions rattle global markets.

title: JCI Opens Higher as Energy Rally Counters War-Driven Volatility
body: JCI rises 0.54% to 8,059 as energy and gold stocks offset geopolitical jitters and rising oil-driven inflation risks.

title: Iran Warns US-Israel Strike Deals Economic Blow to Indonesia
body: Iran says that Tehran has already warned the US and Israel of the possible economic blow of the war on other parts of the world.

title: Energy Prices Surge as Tanker Disruptions, Facility Shutdowns Rattle Global Supply
body: European natural gas futures have spiked 42% following the shutdown of a major supplier of seaborne gas amid the Middle East tensions

title: Fuel Reserves Safe for 20 Days, Govt Says Amid Hormuz Crisis
body: Indonesia moves to secure US oil as Brent hits $82 and Hormuz tensions threaten global supply routes.

title: Strait of Hormuz Tensions Put Pressure on Indonesia’s Energy Security
body: Economists warn oil spikes from Gulf tensions could hit Indonesia’s trade surplus, fuel prices, and inflation.

title: Rupiah Slips to Rp 16,868 Amid Middle East Conflict, Trade Surplus Drops 72.8%
body: Rupiah weakens 81 points to Rp 16,868 as Middle East conflict escalates and Indonesia’s trade surplus shrinks sharply in January.

title: Indonesia Posts 0.68% Monthly Inflation in February, Driven by Food and Gold
body: Indonesia’s inflation reached 4.76% in Feb., while monthly inflation hit 0.68% as food prices and gold jewelry drove consumer costs higher.

title: Indonesia Banks on US Supply as Iran War Triggers Oil Price Hike
body: At home, Indonesian companies are mostly worried about the burgeoning risk premium on oil and gas, Apindo says.

title: Oil Shock and War Fears Drag JCI Down 2.65% on Monday
body: JCI dropped 218 points to 8,016 as US-Israel strikes on Iran drove oil prices higher and rattled Asian markets.

title: Indonesia’s January Trade Surplus Falls to $0.95B as Imports Outpace Exports
body: Indonesia posts a $0.95B trade surplus in January, extending a 69-month streak as imports surge 18.21% and narrow the surplus.

title: Indonesia Gold Stocks Rally as Safe-Haven Demand Surges
body: Indonesia gold stocks rallied as safe-haven demand lifted bullion prices, while the JCI fell 1.6% amid escalating Middle East tensions.

title: Indonesian Palm Oil Export Up 59.63%, But Not Enough to Lift Plunging Surplus
body: Indonesia's trade surplus shrunk to just $950 million in January 2026.

title: BI Ensures Rupiah Stability Amid Iran Conflict
body: Bank Indonesia says it will ensure the rupiah moves in line with fundamentals as escalating Middle East tensions add pressure on markets.

title: KISI Keeps IPO Plans on Track with Deals Up to Rp 3T Amid Market Caution
body: KISI says 7–8 companies remain in its IPO pipeline with deals up to Rp 3T despite cautious market sentiment and global uncertainties.

title: Indonesia Stocks Sink 1.7% After Iran Strike Shakes Markets
body: JCI slides as Iran tensions trigger risk-off sentiment. The index falls 143 points while oil surges and Asian markets open lower.

title: All Indonesian Major Fuel Retailers Increase Pump Prices
body: All major fuel retailers raise gasoline and diesel prices from March 1; subsidized fuels unchanged.

title: Bali Airport Immigration Offers Visa Relief as Iran Conflict Disrupts Flights
body: Bali immigration prepares for overstay cases after Middle East flight cancellations strand 1,600+ passengers.

title: Indonesia’s Largest Airport Cancels 18 Middle East Flights Amid Iran Conflict
body: Soekarno-Hatta Airport cancels 18 Middle East flights as Iran conflict disrupts regional air traffic.
